But never a bad idea as general hygiene to revoke all approvals and minimize active DeFi exposure. Another reason why the @prism_lp model just makes so much sense: reduces attack surface so much.

tweets.text:
Take this from the guy that told you Cushing will tank bottom at the end of June. 

Cushing’s relevance to global oil prices wasn’t what it was like before. 

US has access to landlocked crude from Canada that goes directly to PADD 2. Couple that with 13.7 million b/d of US crude oil production and Cushing is really just a storage hub shifting barrels from one point to the next. 

However, the fact that the market is still pricing US barrels as if we have an abundance is wrong. We are reaching operational minimums, and the market should price the barrels out. 

But it is what it is.
tweets.full_text:
Take this from the guy that told you Cushing will tank bottom at the end of June. 

Cushing’s relevance to global oil prices wasn’t what it was like before. 

US has access to landlocked crude from Canada that goes directly to PADD 2. Couple that with 13.7 million b/d of US crude oil production and Cushing is really just a storage hub shifting barrels from one point to the next. 

However, the fact that the market is still pricing US barrels as if we have an abundance is wrong. We are reaching operational minimums, and the market should price the barrels out. 

But it is what it is.

tweets.text:
Read this if you're wondering why I'm so bearish on Saylor and his Strategy / $STRC ponzi.

He got greedy and overleveraged, which has forced him to break foundational promises and make every $MSTR holder pay for it.

I believe in Bitcoin.

But the blatant worship it's become of one man and one company by "Bitcoin Maximalists" is absurd.

The way so many people try and claim that it's success is dependent on this man pressing the buy button over and over again is also absurd.

Flush the leverage and the idolatry, and then proceed sending everything to new all time highs with haste.

thread_tweets.text:
I'll be the first to admit how much I wanted it to work and refused to think critically about some of the cracks that could happen, but when Saylor started breaking promises and moving the goal posts it forced me to be more objective.

So I spent weeks doing research to find any way possible to try and justify ways he could continue to prop this mess up, but the more time I spent digging the more I realized just how bad everything possibly *could* get... and the article walks through those findings.

Read this if you're wondering why I'm so bearish on Saylor and his Strategy / $STRC ponzi.

He got greedy and overleveraged, which has forced him to break foundational promises and make every $MSTR holder pay for it.

I believe in Bitcoin.

But the blatant worship it's become of one man and one company by "Bitcoin Maximalists" is absurd.

The way so many people try and claim that it's success is dependent on this man pressing the buy button over and over again is also absurd.

Flush the leverage and the idolatry, and then proceed sending everything to new all time highs with haste.

I'll be the first to admit how much I wanted it to work and refused to think critically about some of the cracks that could happen, but when Saylor started breaking promises and moving the goal posts it forced me to be more objective.

So I spent weeks doing research to find any way possible to try and justify ways he could continue to prop this mess up, but the more time I spent digging the more I realized just how bad everything possibly *could* get... and the article walks through those findings.
